pr.title: Group the wiki TU list into progress sections
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Updated tools/wiki_tu.py so generated Translation Units wiki output is grouped by progress state instead of one flat table. The script now buckets TUs into `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`, displays separate `.text %` and `data %` signals, removes the combined percent/checkmark completion column, and applies reviewer-requested formatting fixes for stable anchors, non-wrapping size units, and exactly one leading @ on Discord handles.

key_files.role: Only changed file; rewrites the wiki TU writer output layout, row helpers, bucketing logic, percent formatting, size formatting, and assignee cell formatting.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: tools/wiki_tu.py added 98 deleted 30 hunks 1
raw_diff: diff --git a/tools/wiki_tu.py b/tools/wiki_tu.py
pr_body: `wiki_tu.py write` emitted every translation unit into one flat table

what_changed.text: The flat Translation Units wiki table was replaced with three generated sections: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: This groups the generated page into three sections
raw_diff: in_progress = []; data_left = []; linked = []

raw_diff: print("
## .text in progress
") / print("
## .text matched - data / linking left
") / print("
## Fully linked
")

what_changed.text: Section assignment is based on `metadata.complete` first, otherwise `matched_code_percent >= 100` for data/linking-left rows, otherwise in-progress.
evidence_refs: pr_body table: `.text in progress` criterion `matched_code_percent` < 100; `.text matched - data / linking left` criterion `.text` at 100% but not `complete`; `Fully linked` criterion `metadata.complete`
raw_diff: if unit["metadata"].get("complete"): linked.append(unit) ... elif float(unit["measures"].get("matched_code_percent") or 0) >= 100: data_left.append(unit)

what_changed.text: Active tables now show separate `.text %` and `data %` columns, while fully linked rows use a slim file/text/data table.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Active rows gain separate **`.text %`** and **`data %`** columns

raw_diff: full_header = "File|Matched|Total|.text %|data %|Assignee
Discord|Assignee
GitHub"
raw_diff: slim_header = "File|.text %|data %"

what_changed.text: The old combined percent plus checkmark completion column was removed; completion is represented by section membership.
evidence_refs: pr_body: The combined `%` + checkmark column is removed — section membership now encodes completion
raw_diff removed: percent = ...; linked = ":heavy_check_mark:" if unit["metadata"].get("complete") else ":x:"
raw_diff removed: File|Matched|Total|%|:grey_question:|Assignee...

what_changed.text: Section counts are printed on their own line below each header to keep header anchor URLs stable.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Each section's count is printed on the line below its header so the header anchors stay stable as counts change
raw_diff comment: The count goes on its own line below each header so the header text -- and therefore the section's anchor URL -- stays stable as counts change

what_changed.text: Formatting cleanup changed size strings to use ` ` after humanfriendly formatting and normalized Discord handles to exactly one leading `@`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: size units joined with ` ` so they don't wrap, and Discord handles normalized to exactly one leading `@`
review CHANGES_REQUESTED: The sizes should replace spaces in the strings with ` ` after being run through `humanfriendly`; Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@` symbol
raw_diff: return size.replace(" ", " ")
raw_diff: discord = f"`@{assignee['discord'].lstrip('@')}`"

smart_moves.text: Factored repeated row-generation details into local helpers (`friendly_size`, `percent_cell`, `file_of`, `assignee_cells`, `percent_cells`, `row_full`, `row_slim`) before printing section tables.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: def friendly_size(value: int) -> str
raw_diff: def percent_cell(matched: int, total: int, reported: Optional[float]) -> str
raw_diff: def row_full(unit) -> str / def row_slim(unit) -> str

smart_moves.text: Kept active-table assignee columns at the same indices so existing `read_wiki` round-tripping could continue according to the PR body.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Assignee columns stay at the same indices in the active tables, so `read_wiki` round-trips them as before

raw_diff: full_header keeps Assignee
Discord and Assignee
GitHub after File, Matched, Total, .text %, data %

smart_moves.text: Used `metadata.complete` as the winning bucket condition for fully linked TUs, matching the PR's stated canonical completion signal.
evidence_refs: raw_diff comment: "complete" (fully linked) is the canonical done flag -- it drives the official completed-unit count -- so it wins
pr_body: `Fully linked` | `metadata.complete`

decomp_lessons.text: For decomp progress reporting, separate code/text completion from data/linking completion; a TU with 100% `.text` but incomplete data/linking should not be displayed as finished.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Active rows gain separate **`.text %`** and **`data %`** columns, so a TU that's 100% code but a few percent data is no longer indistinguishable from a finished one
pr_body: `.text matched - data / linking left` | `.text` at 100% but not `complete`
raw_diff: elif float(unit["measures"].get("matched_code_percent") or 0) >= 100: data_left.append(unit)

naming_conventions.text: Generated wiki section names used by this PR: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
evidence_refs: pr_body section table
raw_diff: print("\n## .text in progress\n")
raw_diff: print("\n## .text matched - data / linking left\n")
raw_diff: print("\n## Fully linked\n")

naming_conventions.text: The generated progress columns are labeled `.text %` and `data %`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: separate **`.text %`** and **`data %`** columns
raw_diff: full_header = "File|Matched|Total|.text %|data %|..."
raw_diff: slim_header = "File|.text %|data %"

naming_conventions.text: Discord assignee handles emitted by the wiki tool should have exactly one leading `@`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Discord handles normalized to exactly one leading `@`
review CHANGES_REQUESTED: Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@` symbol
raw_diff: discord = f"`@{assignee['discord'].lstrip('@')}`"

review_feedback.text: Requested removing emoji from section headers because they do not render in the table of contents.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Distinguish text completion from data/linking completion in TU progress views
accepted_candidate_records.text: When presenting translation-unit decomp progress, keep `.text` progress and data/linking completion as separate signals. A TU at 100% code but not `metadata.complete` should be visibly separated from fully linked units.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Active rows gain separate **`.text %`** and **`data %`** columns
pr_body: `.text matched - data / linking left` criterion `.text` at 100% but not `complete`
raw_diff: in_progress/data_left/linked buckets

source_update_candidates.title: Document wiki_tu.py progress-section semantics
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that the generated Translation Units wiki groups rows into `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`, with `metadata.complete` taking precedence over percent values and section counts printed below headers to keep anchors stable.
evidence_refs: pr_body section criteria table
raw_diff comment: complete is the canonical done flag
raw_diff comment: count goes on its own line below each header
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a tooling convention surfaced by the PR, but it should be curated into documentation or source comments only after maintainer review.

source_update_candidates.title: Document assignee formatting rules for wiki round-tripping
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that active TU wiki tables keep assignee columns at stable indices for `read_wiki` round-tripping and emit Discord handles with exactly one leading `@`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Assignee columns stay at the same indices in the active tables, so `read_wiki` round-trips them as before
review: Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@` symbol
raw_diff: assignee_cells(file)
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR supports this as a local tooling behavior; curator review is needed before treating it as a broader project standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer any decompilation match, objdiff validation, or build/test pass from this PR slice; none is loaded.
Do not promote the three wiki section names as global decomp status taxonomy outside the wiki_tu.py reporting context without additional evidence.
Do not infer that reviewer approval specifically endorsed every implementation detail; only the approval state and earlier review requests are evidenced.
